Platyhelminthes > > Polycladida > Cotylea > Pseudocerotidae

Blue-lined flatworm

Pseudoceros concinnus*

Updated Feb 2020

Where seen?

This small pale flatworm elegantly edged in blue with a central blue stripe is regularly encountered on many of our shores, on coral rubble near living reefs. ‘Concinnus’ means ‘elegant’ in Latin.

Features

2-4cm long. There is a pair of pseudotentacles made up of simple folded edges of the body.

Grouped in this entry are flatworms with a body that is white, beige or pale bluish, edged with a bright blue margin and one central blue stripe along the length of the body. The central line ends with a tiny blue circle-dot near the head.

From Rene Ong:

Pseudoceros concinnus
central line is all solid blue

Yellow striped flatworm Pseudoceros sp. 3: central line is yellow with dark blue border

Braided stripe flatworm Pseudoceros sp. 4: resembles Pseudoceros sp. 3 but has a golden-speckled yellow line that looks intermittent by some dull orange and ends in front.

Racing stripe flatworm Pseudoceros bifurcus - Bluish body with central stripe that is white or yellow becoming orange or red near the head, the stripe is edged with a thin dark purple border.

Pulau Jong, Jun 17* Photo shared by Liz Lim on facebook .
Changi, Aug 19* Photo shared by Loh Kok Sheng on facebook .
Pulau Sekudu, Jul 19* Photo shared by Loh Kok Sheng on facebook .

Labrador, Sep 19 Photo shared by Shawne Goh on facebook .
With a solid blue line. Sentosa, May 08
Little blue circle-dot at the head.
With a blue-and-yellow line. Pulau Semakau, Sep 05
Sentosa, Sep 11 Photo shared by Marcus Ng on flickr .
With a blue-and-yellow line. Chek Jawa, Oct 03

*Species are difficult to positively identify without close examination.

On this website, they are grouped by external features for convenience of display.*

Elegant flatworms on Singapore shores

Blue-lined flatworm gliding (Cyrene), Jun 12

Blue-lined flatworm gliding Blue-lined flatworm gliding (Cyrene)

Posted by Singapore Reef Watch on Thursday, 21 June 2012

Acknowledgement

With grateful thanks to Leslie H. Harris of the Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County for comments on this worm and its identification.

References

  • D. M. Bolaños, B. Q. Gan & R. S. L. Ong. 29 Jun 2016. First records of pseudocerotid flatworms (Platyhelminthes: Polycladida: Cotylea) from Singapore: A taxonomic report with remarks on colour variation.(pdf) The Raffles Bulletin of Zoology Supplement No. 34: 130-169 Pp. 130-169.
  • Newman, Leslie and Lester Cannon. 2003. Marine Flatworms: The World of Polyclads . CSIRO Publishing. 97pp.
